categories.introduction: The Vegetable Ontology catalogs canonical vegetables organized by Canonical Culinary Groups — culinary taxonomy aligned with the Pairing Method poster, not botanical classification.
categories.name: Vegetables
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical vegetables by culinary group — plant part, flavor, texture, and seasonality metadata for wine pairing ontology.
categories.summary: Canonical vegetable entities across four culinary groups: Alliums, Green Vegetables, Root Vegetables, and Nightshades.

groups.introduction: Alliums in the Vegetable Ontology — onion, garlic, shallot, and related sulfur-rich culinary vegetables.
groups.name: Alliums
groups.seo_description: Canonical alliums — garlic, onion, shallot, and related vegetables in the PairingMethod Vegetable Ontology.
groups.summary: Alliums — 8 canonical vegetables in the Vegetable Ontology.

groups.introduction: Green vegetables in the Vegetable Ontology — leafy greens, stems, and green pods used in savory cooking.
groups.name: Green Vegetables
groups.seo_description: Canonical green vegetables — kale, spinach, lettuce, and related entities in the PairingMethod Vegetable Ontology.
groups.summary: Green Vegetables — 35 canonical vegetables in the Vegetable Ontology.

groups.introduction: Root vegetables and squash in the Vegetable Ontology — carrots, beets, turnips, and winter squash (excluding potato-class starches).
groups.name: Root Vegetables
groups.seo_description: Canonical root vegetables and squash — carrot, beet, butternut, and related entities in the PairingMethod Vegetable Ontology.
groups.summary: Root Vegetables — 22 canonical vegetables in the Vegetable Ontology.

groups.introduction: Nightshades in the Vegetable Ontology — tomato, eggplant, and pepper-class vegetables in savory culinary context.
groups.name: Nightshades
groups.seo_description: Canonical nightshades — tomato, eggplant, bell pepper, and related entities in the PairingMethod Vegetable Ontology.
groups.summary: Nightshades — 9 canonical vegetables in the Vegetable Ontology.

meta.description: Vegetable Ontology — canonical catalog populated (FOOD-05B). Intrinsic metadata only; relationships deferred to FOOD-05D/E.

canonical_culinary_groups.description: Immutable group names and slugs — do not rename or add peer groups in v1.

culinary_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group value on leaf entities — aligns to immutable group slugs.

culinary_role.description: Intrinsic culinary function for pairing, search, and publication.

flavor_profile.description: Reserved flavor characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-05A; do not populate until post-FOOD-05B curation standards.

domain_exclusions.note: Entities in these poster rows belong to other ontology domains — cross-reference by ID only.

vegetable.description: Canonical vegetable entity with frozen intrinsic metadata.

vegetable_category.description: Top-level vegetable domain hub (single category in v1).

vegetable_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group (Alliums, Green Vegetables, etc.).

schema.taxonomy_note: Groups are culinary, not botanical. See docs/VEGETABLE_GOVERNANCE.md.

vegetables.seo_description: Acorn squash is a ribbed winter squash with nutty flesh — roasted with maple or sage suits medium-bodied whites and Pinot Noir.
vegetables.summary: Acorn squash is a ribbed winter squash with nutty flesh — roasted with maple or sage suits medium-bodied whites and Pinot Noir.

vegetables.seo_description: Artichoke contains cynarin, which can make wine taste sweeter — pair with high-acid whites, Vinho Verde, or oxidative styles that resist the effect.
vegetables.summary: Artichoke contains cynarin, which can make wine taste sweeter — pair with high-acid whites, Vinho Verde, or oxidative styles that resist the effect.
